column3: The difference: Hypabase preserves who scheduled what, with whom, and when in a single retrievable unit. Ask "what time is the team meeting?" and the + :recipient team roles return directly—no cross-referencing fragmented triples. :locus 3pm Thursday Why This Matters Benefit How AMR + Hyperedges Deliver It Query by role: + returns her meetings; add :agent Sarah schedule Precise retrieval to filter :recipient team The meeting's organizer, attendees, and time are stored as one atomic No fragmentation hyperedge Consistent 6 karaka roles map all semantic relationships—no invented relation types extraction per fact PENMAN notation has a defined grammar; broken extractions are caught Parseable output at parse time Mem0's vector similarity can't distinguish "Sarah scheduled" from "Sarah attended." Supermemory's hybrid RAG improves retrieval breadth but still fragments multi-party events into separate triples. Hypabase's role-based hyperedges keep the full event structure intact, delivering 100% accuracy on personalization tasks. Learn more about Hypabase → FAQ Is Mem0 better than Supermemory? Not for retrieval accuracy. Supermemory (85.2%) significantly outperforms Mem0 (49%) on LongMemEval. Mem0 has broader framework coverage and transparent pricing. For the highest accuracy with structured extraction, consider Hypabase (87.4%).
